design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore interconnected map areas, collect items and abilities, solve environment puzzles, defeat bosses to rescue dinosaur companions and unlock new paths
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Non-linear progression gated by item acquisition and boss defeats; player must discover which items unlock which areas
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rescue kidnapped dinosaur companions by exploring a complex world, solving puzzles with versatile tools, and overcoming boss challenges
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radical departure from series formula: eliminates hunger meter entirely, adopts Metroidvania-style non-linear exploration instead of linear stage-based progression, emphasizes item-based world interaction and puzzle-solving over pure platforming skill. Multi-functional items (water gun for puzzles and combat, compass for navigation) create emergent interaction possibilities.

difficulty.lives_system: Life gauge system replacing hunger meter; allows multiple hits; expanded via heart container fragments collected throughout world
difficulty.checkpointing: Complex scrolling map with persistent exploration state; no explicit level restart mentioned
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player controls exploration sequence and can tackle challenges in various orders; life gauge can be expanded through collection, mitigating damage

progression.upgrade_system: Item collection system: weapons (boomerangs, spears), tools (water gun, compass), and heart container fragments. Items serve dual purposes as weapons and puzzle-solving devices
progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss defeats unlock new areas and rescue dinosaur companions; items enable access to previously inaccessible locations
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select (accessible via debug menu)
Item-gated exploration encourages different progression routes
Heart container expansion rewards thorough exploration

world_building_techniques: Complex interconnected map telling implicit spatial narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as narrative checkpoints (each defeat rescues a dinosaur) and progression gates (unlock new map areas). Bosses directly tied to world progression rather than pure challenge
notable_bosses: Multiple boss enemies encountered throughout world
Each boss defeat tied to rescue of specific dinosaur companion
Boss battles feature expanded life gauge mechanic (visible gauge at screen bottom)

faction_indicators: Takashi Meijin protagonist identity (recurring series character)
Five distinct dinosaur companions as rescue objectives
danger_signals: Mysterious entity kidnapping dinosaurs establishes antagonistic force
environmental_storytelling: Complex scrolling map structure conveys world size and interconnectedness
Item placement in environment guides player exploration and puzzle discovery
Location-specific interactions (trees, doors) create place-based gameplay identity

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Takahashi Meijin no Boukenjima IV
